customer_context.department: Network Operations Center (NOC) / Radio Access Network Engineering
pain_points: Dropped calls and interrupted data sessions during mobility events - handoff failures between eNodeBs and gNodeBs especially in high-mobility scenarios
SS7 signaling attacks and rogue network access attempts - vulnerable to location tracking, SMS interception, and fraudulent roaming attacks
MME software bugs and resource exhaustion impacting subscriber capacity - memory leaks and CPU exhaustion degrade performance before failover
Core network split-brain scenarios and signaling storms - network partitions create duplicate authoritative instances causing signaling floods
Radio equipment failures and cell tower outage detection - physical layer failures may not trigger immediate alarms if backup cells provide coverage
Reactive incident response overwhelming help desk operations - lack of predictive alerting causes customer-reported issues to flood help desk before engineering awareness
Service downtime resulting in revenue loss and SLA violations - unplanned EPC and 5G Core outages cost $50K-$200K per hour
HSS database corruption and synchronization issues affecting subscriber authentication - intermittent sync failures between geo-redundant instances
use_cases: Proactive network degradation detection with subscriber impact correlation - detect issues 5-15 minutes before help desk call spikes
MME resource exhaustion and software bug detection - identify memory leaks and resource exhaustion before failover
HSS database health and authentication service monitoring - correlate database performance with authentication success rates
Cell tower handoff cascade failure analysis - identify and visualize handoff failure propagation patterns across adjacent cells
SS7/Diameter security attack detection and roaming fraud prevention - detect signaling attacks through behavioral analysis
Core network split-brain and signaling storm prevention - detect distributed system failures before region-wide outages
customer_context.scale: Multi-region mobile network infrastructure with 2-5 MME hosts per region, 50+ cell sites monitored per cluster, 500+ unique subscribers per incident threshold, millions of signaling events daily across 4G LTE and 5G NR networks
metrics: Subscriber-impacting incident threshold - count of unique IMSIs experiencing service failures
Handoff preparation failure causes - categorized reasons for handoff failures
PDN connection establishment time - end-to-end time from attach request to connectivity
SLA compliance metrics - KPIs against contractual SLAs like 99.95% attach success rate
RRC connection success rate - successful radio resource control establishment percentage
MME CPU utilization by core - per-core and aggregate CPU usage on MME hosts
SCTP association count and state - active associations, churn rate, protocol errors
MME thread pool utilization - worker thread pool metrics for signaling processing
NAS message processing latency - time to process attach, TAU, and service requests
Authentication failure rate - failed authentication attempts as percentage of total attach procedures
IMEI validity check results - device authenticity verification against EIR
Handoff success rate (X2/S1-based) - percentage of successful handoff procedures with latency
Kafka topic lag - consumer lag for signaling event processing pipelines
Revenue-impacting service downtime - calculated downtime for revenue-generating services
GTP-C tunnel creation success rate - bearer establishment success for data sessions
Diameter transaction success rate (S6a interface) - authentication and location update success percentage
Cell availability and operational status - real-time cell operational status from RAN EMS
MME memory utilization and heap statistics - memory consumption and GC metrics
Redis session state cache performance - subscriber session state cache metrics
Rogue network access attempts - attach attempts from unauthorized networks
Unique IMSI cardinality per cell - subscriber count per cell over time windows
SS7/Diameter anomalous message patterns - suspicious signaling indicating potential attacks
S1-MME connection state - active SCTP associations between eNodeBs and MME instances
PRB utilization - downlink and uplink physical resource block usage as capacity indicator
